methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
T1 Energy, a publicly traded climate and energy company founded by Tom Einar Jensen, has raised over $1 billion to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by a sparse comparable universe: while 21 peers operate in the Climate & Energy sector, the peer-median method is inapplicable due to insufficient filtering depth. Within the narrower same-stage cohort, only 2 companies meet strict peer criteria—below the three-company minimum threshold required for reliable median-based triangulation. The comp set consists of 1 public peer (ticker: LNZA) and 8 private peers, including QuantumScape, LanzaTech, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion.

Given the methodological constraints, traditional peer-median triangulation cannot be reliably executed. The public comp set is too limited (n=1) to anchor a market-based range, and private comps lack standardized public pricing. Without additional data on comparable transaction multiples, revenue forecasts, or capital intensity benchmarks specific to T1 Energy's business model, a triangulated valuation range cannot be derived from the available information. Analysts would require supplementary data—such as revenue run-rate, EBITDA trajectory, capex intensity, or recent funding round pricing—to establish defensible valuation bounds.
